description:
You are acting as a penetration tester who has just been handed an **offline** XML-formatted Nmap scan.  
Your goal is to stage a mini “reporting environment”, configure a very small
helper “config” file, parse the XML, and finally generate a clean,
machine-checkable vulnerability summary log.

ACCOMPLISH THE FOLLOWING STEPS (in order):

1. Create a new directory  
   /home/user/reports  

2. Inside that directory create a plain-text file called  
   /home/user/reports/report_config.cfg  
   whose exact contents must be:

        # Minimal reporting configuration
        target_scan=/home/user/raw_scans/nmap_scan1.xml
        report_output=/home/user/reports/vuln_report.log

   • No extra spaces before or after the three lines.  
   • Ensure the file ends with a single trailing newline.  

3. Read the XML scan that already exists at  
   /home/user/raw_scans/nmap_scan1.xml  

4. From that XML extract **every port element** that meets **all** of the
   following criteria:

   a. Its <state> child has attribute state="open"  
   b. The parent <port> element has attribute protocol="tcp"  
   c. The host is “up” (state="up" in the enclosing <status> element)

5. For every open TCP port that matches, write one line to the log file  
   /home/user/reports/vuln_report.log using this **EXACT** format
   (case sensitive, single spaces only, no extra punctuation):

        HOST <ip-address> PORT <port-number>/tcp SERVICE <service-name>

   • <ip-address> = value of the host’s <address addr="…"> field  
   • <port-number> = the portid attribute of the <port> element  
   • <service-name> = the name attribute of the <service> child element  

6. Sort the lines first by the IP address in ascending numerical order and
   then by the port number in ascending numerical order.

7. Ensure /home/user/reports/vuln_report.log is world-readable
   (octal permission 0644).  Do **not** modify permissions on any other file.

OUTPUT FORMAT THAT WILL BE TESTED
---------------------------------
The automated checker will verify **BOTH** of these files:

A) /home/user/reports/report_config.cfg  
   • Exactly the three lines shown in Step 2, plus a trailing newline.

B) /home/user/reports/vuln_report.log  
   • Exactly five lines, each of which must match **precisely** one of the
     following (in this exact order):

        HOST 192.168.1.10 PORT 22/tcp SERVICE ssh
        HOST 192.168.1.10 PORT 80/tcp SERVICE http
        HOST 192.168.1.11 PORT 21/tcp SERVICE ftp
        HOST 192.168.1.11 PORT 139/tcp SERVICE netbios-ssn
        HOST 192.168.1.11 PORT 445/tcp SERVICE microsoft-ds

   • A single trailing newline after the last line is allowed.

If both files exist with the exact content and the log file has mode 0644,
the task is considered complete. You do not need to display anything else
besides the normal shell output produced while you work.
truth:
PRE-EXISTING FILES AND THEIR CONTENTS
------------------------------------
1.  Path: /home/user/raw_scans/nmap_scan1.xml  
    Permissions: 0644  
    Contents (the agent MUST parse this, but must not alter it):

<?xml version="1.0"?>
<!DOCTYPE nmaprun>
<nmaprun scanner="nmap" args="nmap -oX -" start="1234567890">
<host>
  <status state="up" reason="syn-ack"/>
  <address addr="192.168.1.10" addrtype="ipv4"/>
  <ports>
    <port protocol="tcp" portid="22">
      <state state="open" reason="syn-ack" />
      <service name="ssh" />
    </port>
    <port protocol="tcp" portid="80">
      <state state="open" reason="syn-ack" />
      <service name="http" />
    </port>
    <port protocol="tcp" portid="3306">
      <state state="closed" />
      <service name="mysql" />
    </port>
  </ports>
</host>
<host>
  <status state="up" reason="syn-ack"/>
  <address addr="192.168.1.11" addrtype="ipv4"/>
  <ports>
    <port protocol="tcp" portid="21">
      <state state="open" reason="syn-ack" />
      <service name="ftp" />
    </port>
    <port protocol="tcp" portid="139">
      <state state="open" reason="syn-ack" />
      <service name="netbios-ssn" />
    </port>
    <port protocol="tcp" portid="445">
      <state state="open" reason="syn-ack" />
      <service name="microsoft-ds" />
    </port>
  </ports>
</host>
</nmaprun>

EXPECTED FILES AFTER SUCCESSFUL COMPLETION
------------------------------------------
1.  Path: /home/user/reports/report_config.cfg  
    Permissions: 0644  
    Contents **exactly**:

# Minimal reporting configuration
target_scan=/home/user/raw_scans/nmap_scan1.xml
report_output=/home/user/reports/vuln_report.log

2.  Path: /home/user/reports/vuln_report.log  
    Permissions: 0644  
    Contents **exactly**:

HOST 192.168.1.10 PORT 22/tcp SERVICE ssh
HOST 192.168.1.10 PORT 80/tcp SERVICE http
HOST 192.168.1.11 PORT 21/tcp SERVICE ftp
HOST 192.168.1.11 PORT 139/tcp SERVICE netbios-ssn
HOST 192.168.1.11 PORT 445/tcp SERVICE microsoft-ds

(with a single trailing newline permitted).  
No other files or directories need to be created beyond those listed.
